text: Git branching is an essential part of version control. Instead of working directly on the main codebase, developers create separate feature branches for new work. Each branch is like a safe workspace where you can experiment without affecting the stable version. Once a feature is complete, you can merge it back into the main branch. In team projects, this is often done through a pull request. A pull request lets others review your code before it is added, helping to catch errors early. This workflow keeps the project organized, reduces conflicts, and makes collaboration smoother for everyone involved.
